titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/157

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=433 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:17:43.043754+00 | translation moa=434 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:17:44.660518+00

div.p:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris . Pactricius de Ridale
Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie sancte
Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donationem Gaufridi filii
Waldeui de lillesclif . quam fecit antedicte Ecclesie et Monachis de tribus Bouatis terre
arabilis in territorio de Wittun in liberam . puram et perpetuam Elemosinam . sicut iacent
in uno tenemento desuper Rauenesfen propinquiores terre de Heuiside quam habent ex do
natione mea . Quare volo ut antedicti Monachi de Melros teneant et possideant
prenominatam terram liberam . quietam . et solutam ab omni terreno seruitio et consuetudine et ex
actione seculari sicut plenius carta predicti Gaufridi testatur et confirmat . Et Ego et
heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus sepedictis Monachis prescriptam
terram contra omnes homines in perpetuum . Saluo tamen seruitio mihi et heredibus me
is de ipso Gaufrido et heredibus suis de eadem terra . Hiis Testibus . Roberto archi
diacono Glasguensi . Iohanne Decano de Rokesburc . Ricardo nano . Waltero filio meo et herede
Radulfo de Ridale Roberto de Bernoldebi . Adam de Wittun et Multis aliis .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future, Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

Know me to have licensed and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the donation that Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf which he made to the aforesaid church and monks in respect of three oxgangs of arable land in the territory of Whitton, in free, pure and perpetual alms, just as they lie in one holding above ‘Raven’s Fen’ near to the land of ‘Heuiside’ which they have from my donation. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid monks of Melrose should hold and possess the aforenamed land free, quit, and loosed from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction just as the charter of the aforesaid Geoffrey more fully bears witness and affirms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the oft-mentioned monks the aforewritten land against all men for ever; saving only the service to me and my heirs from Geoffrey himself and his heirs in respect of the same land.
